settings.title: Settings
settings.timezone.label: Timezone
settings.timezone.detection_failed: Could not detect timezone from system. Please pick one manually.
settings.timezone.partial_detection: Partial detection — timezone could not be resolved fully. Current value kept.
settings.timezone.help: Affects all timestamps shown in the app.
settings.language.label: Interface language
settings.address.label: My location
settings.address.help: Used on reports and to suggest nearby stations. You can type it manually or click 'Auto-detect' to look it up from your IP (one-time call to ip-api.com — your IP is sent on click only).
settings.address.tz_offer_body: Your IP suggests timezone {tz}. Update the timezone field to match?
header.action.pro_tooltip: Open the Workbench (oscillator, spectrogram, helicorder, particle motion…)
header.theme.tooltip_light: Theme: Light. Click to switch to Dark.
header.theme.tooltip_dark: Theme: Dark. Click to switch to Light.
header.mode.tooltip_standard: Standard VIEW — globe & palette follow the theme. (Does not open the Workbench.)
header.mode.tooltip_professional: Pro VIEW: holographic globe + dark palette. Only changes appearance — for analysis tools use the Workbench button.
replay.field.station_hint: Follows the station selected in the sidebar
replay.error.missing_fields: Please fill Network, Station and Channel.
replay.error.no_data: IRIS has no data for this time window. Try a different time or station.
dialog.add_shake.title: Add LAN Raspberry Shake
dialog.add_shake.label: Label
dialog.add_shake.tcp_timeout: ✗ TCP timeout (5 s) to {host}:{port}. Firewall or wrong IP?
settings.my_shakes.help: Manage your own Raspberry Shake devices on the local network. Each entry can be picked from the Station dropdown in the Workbench.
settings.my_shakes.empty: No LAN Shakes saved yet. Click "Add…" to register one.
settings.reset.heading: Clear cache and reset SeismicGuard
settings.reset.help: Delete all your preferences, favourites, LAN Shakes, usage statistics and any locally cached data. The app will close, and the next time you open it you'll see the welcome flow as if you'd just installed it. Your seismic recordings on disk are not touched.
settings.reset.confirm_body: This will permanently delete all your preferences, favourites, LAN Shakes, usage stats and cached data. The app will close — open it again to start fresh. This cannot be undone. Continue?
settings.reset.partial: Cache cleared with some warnings: {errors}. Closing app…
audio.error.format_unsupported: Audio device does not support this format.
audio.error.buffer_open: Could not open internal audio buffer.
spectrogram.title: Spectrogram
helicorder.title: Helicorder · EHZ · {hours} h × {minutes} min/row
particle.title: Particle motion · N-E plane · {seconds:.1f} s window
globe.loading_subtitle: Loading resources and connecting to USGS…
localizame.heading: Locating you
onboarding.welcome.tagline: Real-time seismic monitoring on your desktop. Watch global earthquakes, listen to live ground motion, and analyse waveforms — all in one app.
onboarding.welcome.privacy: Privacy first. SeismicGuard never tracks you and only uses your timezone (read from the OS) to localise displays.
onboarding.language.heading: Choose your language
onboarding.language.help: You can change this any time in Settings.
onboarding.timezone.heading: Confirm your time zone
onboarding.timezone.help: Used to display earthquake times in local hours. We detected this from your system; pick another if it's wrong.
onboarding.theme.heading: Choose your theme
onboarding.theme.help: Auto switches between light and dark by local hour. You can override any time.
onboarding.layer.heading: Standard or Professional?
onboarding.layer.help: Standard shows a day/night globe. Professional adds a holographic look and forces dark UI — useful for mission-control style monitoring. Switch any time.
onboarding.done.heading: You're all set
onboarding.done.body: SeismicGuard is ready. Click Get started to enter the app. You can always reopen this guide from Help → Run onboarding again.
github.login.intro_body: Link your GitHub account to personalise your profile in SeismicGuard with your username and avatar. We only request read-only access to your public profile — never to your repositories or organisations.
github.login.wait_body: Open GitHub in your browser, paste the code below, and approve the request. We'll detect the approval automatically.
github.login.success_body: Welcome, {name}! You're now signed in as @{login}.
github.login.not_configured: GitHub login is not configured. Set SEISMICGUARD_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID or enter a client ID above.
github.login.error_expired: The code expired before you authorised. Please try again.
profile.stats.title: Usage
profile.activity.title: Recent activity
profile.activity.hint: Your last 50 actions in the app, most recent first. Stored locally — never sent anywhere.
profile.activity.empty: No activity yet. Browse the globe, generate a report or connect a station and it will appear here.
intensity.title: Live intensity
intensity.desc.2: Felt by people at rest on upper floors.
intensity.desc.3: Indoor vibration similar to a truck passing by.
status.ready: Ready. Select a station and click «Connect».
status.no_source_active: No active source. Audio (if any) stopped.
status.stopping_source: ⏹ Stopping source… (may take a few seconds if a handshake was in progress)
status.stations_zero: Stations: 0 — verify access to data.raspberryshake.org and service.iris.edu
status.no_audio_yet: No data to listen to yet — connect a source first.
status.playing_clip: Playing {seconds} s sped × {speed} ({duration:.1f} s of audio).
status.no_data_for_report: No USGS data yet for the report. Wait for the next refresh (≤ 60 s).
status.favorite_not_found: Cannot favorite {id}: not in current data set
status.about: {app} v{version} — open data from Raspberry Shake
status.station_not_found: Station {network}.{code} not found in catalog yet. Try again in a few seconds.
status.station_added: ✓ {network}.{code} added to Workbench ({n}/{cap}). Open the Workbench and click «Connect» to start.
status.station_already_there: {network}.{code} was already in the list; selected.
dialog.shake.title: Raspberry Shake — info only
dialog.shake.body:
{network}.{code}

{site}
Latitude: {lat:.3f}°
Longitude: {lon:.3f}°
Elevation: {elev:.0f} m

Remote Raspberry Shake stations do not expose a public SeedLink server; you can only connect to your own device on the local network (rs.local). LAN-connection support will arrive in a future iteration.
dialog.usgs.title: Connect to {network}.{code}
dialog.usgs.body:
{network}.{code}
{site}

Latitude: {lat:.3f}°
Longitude: {lon:.3f}°
Elevation: {elev:.0f} m

SeedLink server: {host}:{port}
Channels: {channels}
Slot: {slot}/{cap}

Add this station to the Workbench list?
web.dashboard.error.title: Could not load dashboard
web.globe.legend.title: Magnitude
web.globe.error.title: Could not load the globe
report.no_24h_to_chart: No events in 24 h to chart.
report.html.title: SeismicGuard · Seismic activity report
report.html.subtitle: Summary of the last 24 hours + {window} ranking
settings.reset.item.preferences: All app preferences (language, timezone, theme, layer mode)
github.login.client_id_help: Don't have a client ID? Register a free OAuth App on GitHub — it takes 30 seconds and only needs a name.
error.dataselect.contact: IRIS dataselect: could not be reached: {error}
source.seedlink.dns_failed: ✗ DNS failed for «{host}»: {error}. Check the hostname or your connection.
source.seedlink.tcp_timeout: ✗ TCP timeout ({seconds} s) to {endpoint}. Likely a firewall/VPN blocking the port.
source.seedlink.tcp_ok: TCP OK ({ms} ms). Starting SeedLink handshake…
source.seedlink.handshake: SeedLink handshake (may take 10–60 s if the server is congested)…
source.seedlink.subscribed: ⏳ Subscribed to [{selectors}]. Waiting for first packet…
particle.no_horizontal:
No horizontal data (N/E)
Only the vertical (Z) channel is streaming
replay.event_loaded: Event window loaded — click Load, then theoretical P/S will overlay.
dialog.replay_event.title: Review this earthquake?
replay.export_none: Nothing to export yet (download a trace / place P-S picks first).
replay.band.tooltip: Band = different sensor / sample-rate at the SAME station. The station code follows the sidebar selection; here you only pick which instrument channel to load.
replay.band.bh: BH · Broadband 20–40 Hz — P/S body waves (default)
replay.band.hh: HH · High-rate broadband 100 Hz — high-freq / local
replay.band.lh: LH · Long-period 1 Hz — surface waves / teleseisms
replay.rotate_tooltip: Rotate horizontals N/E → Radial/Transverse by back-azimuth (separates P-SV from SH). Needs an event: enter Replay by clicking an earthquake on the globe.
spectrum.title: Power spectrum (PSD) — selection
spectrum.hint: Select a segment (yellow box) to see its spectrum
events.title: Earthquake catalog (USGS)
events.hint: Double-click an event to review it in Replay
status.kiosk_on: Kiosk mode — press F11 or Esc to exit
replay.arrivals_outside: Theoretical P/S fall outside the window (Δ={dist}°, first ≈+{secs}s) — increase duration
events.nearest_hint: Double-click a station to review the event with it
local.title: Local data
local.recordings: Triggered recordings — raw waveforms auto-saved by the STA/LTA detector (this machine). Double-click to open.
local.catalog: Saved catalog — reviewed P/S picks you saved from Replay ('Save to catalog'), as QuakeML.
replay.click_load_hint: Event ready — click Load to fetch the waveform ↑
replay.window_autoset: Window auto-set to cover P/S (Δ={dist}°) — click Load
replay.field.station_event: Event station (independent of the live station)
status.opened_in_workbench: Opened in the Workbench window 🔬 (may be behind this one)
local.confirm_delete_rec: Delete recording file '{name}'? This cannot be undone.
local.confirm_delete_cat: Remove this event from the local catalog?
mine.title: My collection
mine.fav_no_coords: This favorite has no coordinates and is no longer in the feed — can't review.
replay.catalog_no_picks: This catalog entry has no saved picks.
error.fdsn.too_many: Too many earthquakes match (over 20,000). Narrow the time range, raise the minimum magnitude, or pick a region.
error.fdsn.bad_request: The historical query was rejected. Check the dates and filters.
error.fdsn.contact: Could not reach the USGS historical catalog: {error}
hist.hint: Query the full USGS catalog (~1900→today). Narrow by time, magnitude and region to stay under the 20,000 limit.
events.showing_capped: Showing the {shown} most recent of {total} — refine filters to narrow.
web.dashboard.pro.insufficient: Not enough events for this analysis
report.pro.insufficient: Not enough events for statistical analysis
report.an.title: SeismicGuard · Seismic analysis report
report.an.subtitle: Statistical summary of the selected catalog
dashboard.count_over: ≈ {n} events — over 20,000, narrow time/region or raise min magnitude
report.an.findings.title: Findings
report.an.methods.title: Methods & data provenance
report.an.warn.title: Caveats
report.an.params.title: Query parameters
report.an.findings.overview: {n} events of M{mmin}–{mmax} over {days} days in {region}.
report.an.findings.energy_dom: Energy release is dominated by the largest event (M{m}, {pct}% of total).
report.an.findings.energy_dist: Energy release is spread across several events (largest = {pct}% of total).
report.an.findings.mcb_stable: Completeness and b-value appear stable over time.
report.an.findings.mcb_var: Completeness/b-value vary over time — interpret rate changes with care.
report.an.findings.spatial_clustered: Seismicity is spatially clustered (densest cell ≈ {pct}% of events).
report.an.findings.spatial_dispersed: Seismicity is spatially dispersed (densest cell ≈ {pct}% of events).
report.an.warn.bvalue: b-value may be unreliable: catalog truncated near Mc / narrow magnitude span (M≥{minmag}). For a robust b, include events down to true completeness (≈M2–3).
report.an.warn.magtype: Magnitude types are not verified — the b-value assumes a single magnitude scale; mixing scales biases it.
report.an.warn.energy: Total energy is dominated by the few largest events.
report.an.methods.b: b-value — Aki–Utsu MLE for M ≥ Mc; ± via Shi & Bolt
report.an.methods.section: Cross-section — PCA projection perpendicular to the seismicity trend
report.an.cap.gr: Cumulative N(≥M) vs M (log); dashed line = b-value fit above Mc.
report.an.cap.energy: Cumulative event count (N, left axis) and seismic energy (J, right axis) vs time.
report.an.cap.mcb: Mc and b-value in moving windows; shaded band = ±1σ on b.
report.an.cap.spatial: Event count per lon×lat cell; color scales with count.
report.an.cap.section: Depth vs distance perpendicular to the seismicity trend (PCA).
report.an.cap.interevent: Times between consecutive events (log axis); broad ≈ random, short-peaked ≈ clustered.
report.live.summary.title: Situation summary
report.live.sum.overview: In the last {window}: {n} earthquakes, up to M{max}.
report.live.sum.none: No earthquakes recorded in the last {window}.
report.live.notes.title: Sources & notes
report.live.notes.preliminary: Real-time magnitudes and locations are preliminary and may be revised by the network.
report.live.cap.timeline: Events over the last 24 h; dot size and color scale with magnitude.
